source_pr.title: server/tests: fix flaky test TestIssue57531
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #65228

Problem Summary:

### What changed and how does it work?

In [TestIssue57531](https://github.com/pingcap/tidb/blob/c51109cbbdec00619066e8afaf5347f926d77eea/pkg/server/tests/commontest/tidb_test.go#L3441), a time-based assertion was introduced to check if the killed session is not shown:

```
		time.Sleep(10 * time.Millisecond)

		// the `select sleep(300)` is killed
		ts.RunTests(t, nil, func(dbt *testkit.DBTestKit) {
			rsCnt = 0
			rs := dbt.MustQuery("show processlist")
			for rs.Next() {
				rsCnt++
			}
			require.Equal(t, rsCnt, 1)
		})
```

When there's a high workload, a sleep of 10 ms is not enough (I can always reproduce the failure when running with 100% CPU usage). I think it is reasonable to loosen this time to 5s for such a situation and still treat it as 'immediate' compared with `select sleep(300)`. 

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- None(test only)

Documentation

- None(test only)

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: `TestIssue57531` was flaky because it used a fixed short `time.Sleep(10ms)` to wait for a killed session to disappear from `show processlist`, which is not reliable under CPU/load jitter.
failure_signature: TestIssue57531: intermittent failure where `show processlist` still shows the killed session (row count != 1) after a fixed short sleep
root_cause_explanation: The test depended on timing: it killed a `select sleep(300)` session and then slept for only 10ms before asserting the killed session was no longer in `show processlist`. Under load, session termination and processlist updates are not guaranteed to complete within 10ms, so the assertion could fail nondeterministically. The PR fixes the flakiness by replacing the fixed sleep with `require.Eventually(...)` (up to 5s with polling), which waits until the processlist reflects the killed session state.
fix_pattern: Replace fixed `time.Sleep(...)` synchronization in tests with `require.Eventually(...)`/polling and a reasonable timeout to handle load-dependent timing jitter (e.g., wait for `show processlist` to reflect killed sessions).

evidence: evidence: server/tests: fix flaky test TestIssue57531
